Replay an Incident
Zero config. First insight in 60 seconds.

Mergen shows you why
your production broke.

And remembers it forever. AI made writing code cheap — understanding production failures became the bottleneck. Mergen turns incidents and human overrides into queryable operational memorythat prevents repeat failures.

See it in 60 seconds →Star
npx mergen-server
✓ No configuration required✓ 50 sample incidents pre-loaded✓ Override-based learning✓ Semantic safety & blast-radius check✓ Platt-calibrated probability model

31/33Correct classifications2 known false positives
< 60sTime to first insightzero config required

mergen — autonomous incident loop

Watch: 60s Quick Start

01 // The Difference

Before Mergen.
After Mergen.

Without Mergen

0m
PagerDuty fires
Engineer wakes up. Opens laptop.
5m
Check logs
Grep through millions of lines across services.
15m
Check dashboards
Correlate metrics across 5 different tabs.
30m
Ask Slack
"Who deployed last?" "Is the DB down?"
45m
Guess root cause
Apply a fix based on intuition. Hope it works.
60m+
Watch and wait
Monitor dashboards for another 15 min to confirm.

With Mergen

0m
PagerDuty fires
Mergen receives the webhook.
2s
Analyze telemetry
Correlates logs, traces, and infra signals.
5s
Check operational memory
Matches against past incidents and human overrides.
10s
Generate validated fix
Produces a remediation plan at ≥85% confidence.
1m
Resolve or recommend
Executes (autopilot) or posts fix for approval.
2m
Audit trail posted
Full root cause + actions logged to Slack.

Result: The engineer wakes up to a resolved incident and a full audit trail — not a 3am fire drill. Every action is logged and reversible.

03 // How It Works

Four steps from alert
to resolution.

STEP 1

Connect your production stack

Ingest signals from PagerDuty, OpenTelemetry, Docker, Kubernetes, and optionally Datadog. No agent required.

STEP 2

Detect and understand incidents

When an alert fires, Mergen correlates telemetry across services, identifies the likely root cause, and matches it against past incidents and overrides.

STEP 3

Apply operational memory

Mergen checks what your team did last time — past fixes, human overrides, known failure patterns — before generating a validated remediation plan.

STEP 4

Resolve or recommend

Shadow mode: suggestion only. Assisted: recommended fix + approval. Autopilot: safe execution within constraints. Every action is logged and reversible.

PAGERDUTY

Incident Alerts

Webhooks · Severity V3

OPENTELEMETRY

Traces + Metrics

OTLP HTTP · any language

DOCKER

Container Logs

stdout/stderr · any stack

APM Spans

Optional · blame attribution

05 // Evaluation

Evaluated before
you ever use it.

We built a regression eval harness before shipping v1. Every PR that touches detection logic must pass this suite — 33 real incident scenarios, 10 infrastructure failure classes. When we say 94% accuracy, that number is reproducible and falsifiable.

Overall accuracy
94%
31 of 33 incidents classified correctly
Corpus size33 incidents
Correct31 / 33
False positives2 (probe errors)
Last eval runJun 16, 2026
Failure classFixturesAccuracy
DB Connection Pool
5/5
100%
OOM Kill
5/5
100%
Rate Limit Cascade
3/3
100%
Certificate Expiry
3/3
100%
Disk Pressure
3/3
100%
Service Unavailable
3/3
100%
Slow Query
2/2
100%
Downstream Latency
3/3
100%
Queue Backlog
3/3
100%
Upstream Error
1/1
100%
False positive (probe/scrape errors)
Fires on /health and /metrics — human override needed
0/2
0%

Why this matters: Most observability tools are evaluated by the engineers who built them, on the incidents they chose. Mergen ships a public eval harness — the same suite that gates every release. The 2 failures are documented: the detector fires on liveness probe and Prometheus scrape errors when it shouldn't. Fix is in the roadmap; hiding it is not.

View Full JSON Baseline →
02 // The Problem

AI increased deployment speed.
Production systems didn't get safer.

Modern teams ship code faster than ever. But operational understanding is still human — reconstructed from scratch every time something breaks.

01

Context is lost at deploy time

Engineers reconstruct from scratch under pressure

When a human spent three days on a complex fix, the context lived in their head. When incidents happen now, engineers grep logs, jump dashboards, and ask Slack — rebuilding understanding under pressure, at 3am.

02

Knowledge is trapped in people

Slack threads and individual memory don't scale

Past incidents live in individual engineers, old Slack threads, and runbooks nobody updates. When the person who fixed it last time is on holiday, the team starts from zero. Every repeat incident is a failure of memory, not a failure of engineering.

03

AI-generated code ships silent risk

Fast code, slow understanding

AI coding tools generate production logic in seconds. Nobody documented it. Nobody knows the failure modes. Connection pool exhaustion, timeout cascades, silent regressions — these only manifest in production, and only the system that watched them happen can explain them.

The missing layer is not code generation. It is operational memory — for systems that now evolve faster than humans can track.

Talk to us
04 // Core Systems

Memory that compounds
with every incident.

01

Operational Memory & Calibration

Every resolved incident generates a structured record. To resolve AI overconfidence, Mergen uses Platt scaling calibration to convert raw heuristics into empirical probabilities based on your system's verdict corpus—ensuring autonomous actions run only at calibrated safety levels.

MRG
Mergen APP 3:17 PM
✅ Incident #402 resolved autonomously
Audit Trail Summary
• Root Cause: DB Connection Pool exhaustion (api-service)
• Confidence: 91% (matches pattern: stuck_idle_connections)
• Action: Flushed idle pools & increased capacity (max_idle: 5 → 20)
• Validation: Error rate 14% → 0.02% (confirmed 3:18 PM)

02

Override-Based Policy

Every time your team overrides Mergen's recommendation, that decision becomes machine-readable policy. After six months: your Friday settlement windows, compliance holds, and on-call preferences form your specific operational DNA—enforcing invariants before autopilot triggers.

03

Agent Blunder Log (CISO Insurance)

Your audit trail for AI-driven operations and your financial insurance policy against the Agent Outage Tax. Mergen records every blocked action—allowlist blocks, corpus halts, planning gates, and semantic blocks—so security teams can quantify prevented downtime.

04

Semantic Safety Gates

AI agents generate code in seconds but create silent risks. Mergen red-teams commands using a local semantic safety engine, assessing action risk, blast radius, and context limits instead of relying on simple string/regex allowlists.

auth_middleware.ts — Mergen Contextmcp.json
// Mergen: Historical Context found
⚠️ This file was modified in Incident #388 (OOM Kill).
Reason: Recursive token validation on nested JWTs.
Constraint: Do not increase stack depth > 4.
export const validateToken = (token: string) => {
  // checking depth...

05

Measurable MTTR & Progress

Show the before/after in a board deck. The impact report isolates Mergen's context-assisted value, contrasting autonomous vs. manual MTTR and tracking developer engagement rates to demonstrate organic workflow integration.

06

Staged Trust Progression

De-risk automation step-by-step. Run in shadow mode for 30 days to build a track record, then gradually promote execution permission tiers from service restarts, to rollbacks/pins, to full autonomous action.

04 // Interactive Sandbox

Test the detector
logic.

Select Scenario

Adjust Telemetry Inputs

Idle Connections85% (Critical)
Error Rate Spike14.2% (High)
// Telemetry context pack:pg_stat_activity count=85/100, wait_event=ClientRead
Adjust parameters on the left, then click "Run Detector" to see Mergen's diagnostic check in action.
Causal Rule Match:✓ MATCHED (Armed)

06 // Getting Started

First insight in 60 seconds.
Pilot success: 1 real incident analyzed.

01
zero config · Node.js 18+

See it in 60 seconds

Run one command. Mergen starts a local server, loads 50 sample incidents from public postmortems, and immediately shows you a root cause analysis. No PagerDuty, no OTLP, no IDE setup required.

npx mergen-server

Opens http://localhost:3000/demo — click "Trigger P1 Incident" or ask a question in the chat tab.

02
PagerDuty · OTLP · Docker

Connect Your Stack (optional)

When you're ready to switch from sample incidents to real production data, connect one source. Start with Docker logs — it requires zero configuration and works immediately.

# Docker logs (easiest — works immediately)
curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:3000/watchers/docker

# PagerDuty → Service → Webhooks → https://your-host:3000/webhooks/pagerduty

# OTLP (any language — one env var)
OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://127.0.0.1:3000 node app.js

Pilot success condition: Mergen correctly analyzes 1 real incident in your environment.

03
Claude Code · Cursor · VS Code

Add to Your AI IDE

Register Mergen as an MCP server. The tools — triage_incident, analyze_runtime, validate_fix — appear automatically in your IDE.

# Guided setup (detects your IDE automatically)
mergen-server setup

# Or manually — Claude Code
claude mcp add mergen --transport stdio -- node "$(pwd)/server/dist/index.js"

Ask: "What caused the last incident?" — Mergen answers with root cause + fix hint.

04
optional · ≥85% confidence gate

Enable Autopilot

Set MERGEN_AUTOPILOT=true to let Mergen execute fixes autonomously. Starts in shadow mode for 30 days — builds a track record before it acts.

MERGEN_AUTOPILOT=true mergen-server start
# Shadow mode (observe only, no execution):
# omit MERGEN_AUTOPILOT — triage_incident still available on demand

Every blocked action is recorded in the Agent Blunder Log. Audit trail at ~/.mergen/audit.log.

Full Install Guide →Define your pilot success criteria →
